Mabrow Pigherder is the owner of a house located in the town of Hearthstone, situated near the commonwater and the breakwall.

Natir is a tall servant from the coastal city of Dalilak who serves as the steward to the late Miliv and later to Roshone. He is accompanied by his stern wife, Alaxia, and is responsible for managing the household affairs and quieting the crowd during gatherings.
Dalilak is a large coastal city located to the north, from which the steward Natir was brought after the death of Miliv.
Cabine is a character described as one of the ten fools who acts like a child despite being an adult.
Rillir is the son of Roshone who enters the kitchens chatting with a companion, criticizing his father's provincial habits and expressing a desire to take him on hunts.

Rillir Roshone is the son of Roshone, a lighteyes master who is described as corrupt and provincial. He is the antagonist Kal addresses, claiming he will not be bullied by Rillir just as his father is not bullied by Kal's father.
A social caste or status within the city of Salas that denotes a low position, comparable to being demoted to the tenth dahn, and prevents individuals from inheriting through marriage.
Gashash-son-Navammis is a character whose translation is considered uncertain by the narrator, who intends to verify a quote attributed to him in the fourteenth book of Seld.
Goshel is a slave who was led in a rebellion by the protagonist that ended in slaughter, an event the protagonist survived but feels guilty about having failed.
The Envisagers are a subject of stories and legends that Teft considers fool's tales and idle dreams, though he acknowledges they possess these narratives and legends.
Alezarv is a character who possesses the ability to cross a significant distance in a single heartbeat, a feat that would normally take more than four months to travel on foot.
Calinam is the author of the folktale 'Among the Darkeyed,' which is recorded on page 102 and features stories of instantaneous travel and the Oathgates.
Oathgates are a form of instantaneous travel mentioned in folktales recorded by Calinam.

A belief system that posits morality is distinct from human ideals and exists as a separate whole to be approached by mortals. It asserts that removing evil is ultimately moral, thereby justifying the destruction of evil men.
Sur Kamar is a person who attended a feast in the narrator's father's name, an event the narrator felt compelled to attend despite the travel required.
Bluebar is a brand of jam produced by Brother Kabsal, which is given to a recipient with a note suggesting that enjoying it indicates the recipient is mysterious, reserved, and thoughtful.
Ambrian is the author of the book 'The Last Desolation', as indicated by the attribution in the provided text.
Idolir is a person mentioned alongside Treff in a conversation with Kaladin regarding a crew of men who have lost track of time.
Treff is a member of a crew of men who, along with Idolir, were lost to the group for several weeks.

Marabethia is a location situated beside the sea in the northern Selay lands, known for its inhabitants' passion for public debate at city intersections and their habit of carrying overripe fruit to counter arguments.
Brightlord Rillir is the son of Brightlord Roshone who is being treated by Lirin for severe injuries sustained from bony spears, including a crushed leg and an object protruding from his abdomen.
Alds is a townsman who was on a hunt with Milp but was left behind by Roshone after the party returned with two wounded lighteyes.
Milp is a townsman who was on a hunt with Alds but was left behind by Roshone after the party returned with two wounded lighteyes.
Cormshen is a character who speaks about the loss of light and the burning of skin, as referenced on page 104.
Innia is a character who records children's folktales, specifically describing the Voidbringers as recurring threats that arrive like a highstorm and referring to their appearances as the Desolation.

The Order of Stonewards is a faction whose members, such as the Radiant Talatin, are described as being able to transform like shadows, though the specific nature of this ability is not detailed in the provided text.
New Natanan is a location referenced in records collected by hunters, explorers, and tradesmen who were searching for a river passage to reach it.

Elhokar Kholin is the king's son and heir who rules a feast in his father's absence. He is criticized by his superiors for allowing his sister to use a fabrial, a device considered trivially important compared to Shardblades.
Vorin Roshar is the collective name for the civilizations and regions across the planet, including Alethkar and Kharbranth, where the repercussions of stealing a fabrial could be felt.
Brightness Hashal is a character who speaks with a Kholinar accent and acts as the new captain of a squad, claiming to do so in the name of her husband, Brightlord Matal.

Brightlord Matal is the husband of Brightness Hashal and serves as the new captain of the group. He is described as a tall but spindly man with delicate hands who has not practiced with his sword much.
Matal is a tall, spindly lighteye who serves as a commander for Sadeas's soldiers and acts as a liaison to Hashal's husband, providing orders to Kaladin's crew before the final assault.
Durk is a character who served as a mentor to Kaladin, offering him advice on gambling and superstition, and previously referred to Kaladin as a natural-born soldier.
Yulay is a location where groups of people discuss the Radiants and express a desire for their return.
Highmarshal Amaram is a renowned soldier and head of Alethi defense in the area, who appears to welcome a warlord and leads an army of six thousand men preparing for battle.
Waber is a character who is tasked by Roshone to gather the townspeople for a full town meeting and is present in the square to help erect a canopy as the rain intensifies.
Alaxia is the stern wife of Natir, the steward of Roshone, who assists Amaram in recruiting volunteers for his undermanned forces by reading names from a list.
Callins is a farmer who addresses a noble as Brightlord and questions the necessity of a specific duty.
Abry is a character who speaks in the narrative, agreeing with a previous statement.
Agil is a character listed by Alaxia alongside Caull, identified specifically as the son of Marf.
Marf. Caull is the father of Agil, a character listed by Alaxia alongside his son and grandfather Taleb.
Taleb is the father of Caull, who is listed alongside Agil, son of Marf, on a list read by Alaxia.
Habrin is the son of Arafik, listed alongside Jorna and Tien as individuals introduced by Alaxia.
Arafik is the father of Habrin, who is listed alongside other individuals such as Jorna and Tien in a group introduced by Alaxia.
Jorna is a character introduced as the son of Loats, listed alongside other individuals such as Habrin and Tien during a formal introduction.
Loats is the father of Jorna, who is listed alongside other individuals such as Habrin and Tien in a sequence of names.
Hearth-stone is the location where Roshone remains trapped after seeking revenge, despite the death of his son.

Yelig-nar, also known as Blightwind, is a character who possesses the ability to speak like a man but is accompanied by the wails of those he consumes. He is a destructive force responsible for slaughtering wordsmen and breaking into the chancery, contributing to the near-destruction of the world and the loss of half the members of many families.
Blightwind is the name given to Yelig-nar, a being capable of speaking like a man whose voice is often accompanied by the wails of those he consumed.
Traxil is the author of a quote regarding the Unmade, which is considered a primary source though its authenticity is doubted.
Klade is a character who frequently asks about the Parshendi's music and expresses a carefree attitude toward highstorms destroying their crude crem and stone buildings.
Stormwarden Matain is a scholar who accompanied the king and dictated a record describing the first Parshendi hunting party encountered by the group.
Matain is a source of an account regarding a man's interest in the Parshendi, their language, society, and music before he could have known about their Shardblades.

The Vanrial is an order of artists who reside on the slopes of the Silent Mount in Jah Keved and are known for singing ancient songs that may have been written by the Heralds. The city of Vanrial is also referenced as a place abandoned before the Lost Radiants turned against mankind, with its history preserved only in fragments.
Tifandor is a person whose biography is referenced and sought by Shallan, and whose name is mentioned by another individual standing at the room's exit.
Aharietiam is the name of the final Desolation, a cataclysmic event led by the Heralds that cast the Voidbringers back into the Tranquiline Halls and ended the Heraldic Epochs.
The ardentia is a faction within the social hierarchy where membership determines one's status, with leaving the group resulting in severe demotion and loss of resources.
Veden City is a location that the narrator was scheduled to visit for dinner, situated along a route that includes the city of Kholinar and the fortress of Urithiru.

Tivbet is a person whom the narrator insists on visiting in Kholinar before traveling to Veden City, despite the growing unreasonableness of tariffs through Urithiru and the Radiants showing their true nature.

The Radiants are a group of individuals whose true nature has been revealed, distinct from the home of Urithiru which some mistakenly believe to be their birthplace. They are subjects of discussion regarding ideals and stories, with characters questioning why men must rely on dreams and fabrications for inspiration rather than embodying such traits.
Terxim is an individual whose autobiography was once associated with the Palanaeum, though only a single page of his account remains available.
The Sea of Spears is a shallow inland sea located north and west of Alethkar, characterized by broken fingers of rock that jut above the water's surface.
Tanavast is a location referenced in the phrase 'Child of Tanavast,' which appears to be a title or lineage associated with the character Kaladin.

Babatharnam is a land known for its peculiar trees that lie down when a highstorm approaches and its inhabitants, the Babath, who have distinctive vein patterns on their skin. The region is currently experiencing unrest and is considered dangerous to visit due to high rates of assassination, exile, and death on the battlefield.
Babab is a name mentioned by Moash in a conversation, though the specific identity or nature of Babab is not defined within the provided text.
Babath is a location where a unique system of rule exists, granting office to the elderly and designating the oldest ruler as the Most Ancient.
A title used to describe individuals who visit various places around the land and tell others of what they have seen, a role that Rock suggests is the correct name for the character Kaladin.
Delp is a member of Kaladin's bridge crew who was wounded in the neck by an arrow during a run, leaving the team with only twenty-eight bridge-capable members.
A term used by Kaladin to address one of the bridgemen, indicating that the group consists of soldiers from a specific faction rather than being a single individual.
A lordling character who appears to be only a few years older than Kaladin.
Reral Makoram is a Brightlord who commands the Sixth Battalion and is contacted by a Shardbearer to inform him that Adolin Kholin cannot attend a scheduled meeting.
The Sixth Battalion is a military unit commanded by Brightlord Reral Makoram, to whom Kaladin is instructed to deliver a message regarding Highprince Adolin Kholin's absence from a scheduled meeting.
Adolin Kholin is a Shardbearer who sends a message to Kaladin stating that he will not attend a scheduled meeting, instructing Kaladin to inform Brightlord Reral Makoram and arrange a new time.
Merem is a concept or value associated with the burgundy and dark green banner of Amaram's standard, paired with khakh to represent honor and determination.
Khakh is a soldier serving under Brightlord Amaram, whose name is paired with Merem on a burgundy and dark green banner featuring a white-spine glyphpair with upraised tusks.
Nalem is a sergeant who serves under the squadleader Gare, accompanying him alongside another sergeant named Korabet.
Korabet is one of the two sergeants who follow the squadleader Gare.
Galan is a character who identifies a new recruit named Cenn to Kaladin, who has just joined the group the day before.

The Nightwatcher is an enigmatic force that appears to toy with the enemy, occasionally setting them as allies against Amaram's troops or turning them against one another in a forbidden game of chance. The entity is also a source of visions that may originate from it, the Voidbringers, or something else entirely, leaving observers unsure of whether to trust them.
Ven is the chief of the surgeons, a tall man with a bulbous nose who oversees the medical pavilion and accepts bribes to prioritize certain soldiers for treatment.
Larn is a character who fought alongside Kaladin and reacted with surprise when Kaladin broke formation to dodge an enemy attack.
Ko-rater is a person who is ordered by Kaladin to return with the boy, alongside Cyn, while Dallet holds the position against Amaram's advancing line.

Toorim is a short man who serves as a subsquad member under Kaladin, having been rescued from another squad by him. He covertly searches for spheres during a battle and reports to Kaladin that the fallen lighteyes either did not bring any spheres or has them hidden under his breastplate.
Norby is the captainlord of the group Kaladin is serving with, whom Kaladin intends to summon to verify their kill.
Lyndel is a character who was trampled by the Shardbearer after Cyn was cut down, resulting in her face being smashed in.
Raksha is a member of Kaladin's squad who is killed by the Shardbearer alongside Navar and four others, leaving only four survivors.
Hab is a soldier serving in Kaladin's squad who survives an initial attack only to be killed by lighteyed officers alongside his comrades Alabet, Reesh, and Coreb.
Reesh is a member of Kaladin's squad who is among the four survivors present when lighteyed officers enter, only to be cut down first during the confrontation.
Alabet is a member of Kaladin's squad who, along with Hab, Reesh, and Coreb, is killed by lighteyed officers while attempting to defend himself with a knife.
Coreb is a character who addresses Kaladin as a Shardbearer and offers him an item, which Kaladin subsequently gives back to Coreb.
A military encampment where thousands of soldiers line up, including squads led by figures such as Cenn and Kaladin.

An ideological group mentioned alongside heretics, Vorins, and Maakians as being equally closed-minded to Jasnah, who argues that true wisdom requires acknowledging the virtue in adversaries regardless of their affiliation.
Mungam is an individual referred to by Shallan as a 'tyrant' who is currently being argued with by Kabsal in the presence of a nurse.
Allahn is the author of a ketek poem that is frequently quoted in reference to Urithiru, though some mistakenly believe the poem indicates the Radiants were born there.
A type of spirit that does not pull Syl downward like other spirits, allowing her to walk with her hands clasped behind her back.
Extes is a man who was tortured daily by having his arms torn off after sacrificing his son to the Voidbringers in exchange for knowledge of his death date.
Rianal is a distant western location from which the bridgeman Drehy originates, as indicated by his faint accent.
Varth is a soldier and squad leader who commands a group of men, including Kaladin, and is the target of Kaladin's desire for retribution.
A title given to Kaladin by his men, referring to a past version of himself who possessed confidence and strength, which he fears he may no longer be able to reclaim.

Lanacin the Surefooted is a person mentioned by the narrator as an individual who killed a Shardbearer, serving as a comparison to Evod Markmaker regarding the narrator's own achievement of killing one.
Evod Markmaker is a Shardbearer mentioned by the narrator as one of the few individuals who has killed a Shardbearer, alongside Lanacin the Surefooted.
Restares is a person whose name is invoked by Amaram as the correct choice for a difficult decision regarding the Shards, representing a course of action deemed necessary for the good of Alethkar.

Baxil is a character who serves as a companion to Av, displaying nervousness as he moves through a lavish palace corridor with a bag of tools. He is identified as being of the Emuli, a group characterized by dark skin and hair.
Emuli is a term used to describe a person with dark skin and hair, specifically referring to Av, who is contrasted with the mistress who is not Emuli despite sharing similar physical traits.

Makabaki is a term used to describe a person from southwestern Roshar, characterized by dark skin and black hair, as exemplified by the bridgeman Sigzil. The text also notes that a mysterious mistress in the hallway possessed features that were not quite Makabaki, despite having dark skin and long black hair.
Ashno of Sages is a wealthy and holy man residing in Emul, who owns hundreds of servants and lives in a palace with a new guest wing.
Sages is the name of a wealthy and holy man in the city of Emul who owns a palace with hundreds of servants.

Emul is the kingdom containing the ancient capital city of Sesemalex Dar, which is said to have been named by Jezrien. The city is the home of Ashno of Sages, one of the richest and holiest men in the kingdom, who employs hundreds of servants.
The Hallowed Hall is a location within a lavish palace where wealthy individuals place images of their Kadasix for reverence, featuring artwork such as a gold leaf painting of Epan, Lady of Dreams.
Kadasix are images or portraits placed in the Hallowed Hall by wealthy individuals for reverence, as depicted in paintings such as the one showing Epan, Lady of Dreams.
Epan is the subject of a painting depicting the Lady of Dreams, displayed in the Hallowed Hall as an image of reverence for a wealthy man's Kadasix.

Geranid is a woman who works on equations and studies spren in a small stone room on a tiny Reshi island. She is accompanied by Ashir, who conducts experiments involving spices and a rare Shin fruit on a fabrial hotplate.
The Physical Realm is the state of existence where the body must be fed, distinct from the separate state where the spirit resides.

Shadesmar is a location that exists within the Cognitive Realm, where visitors can potentially eat food that corresponds to what the realm sees itself as being. It is a place of significant danger, as evidenced by the peril of visiting it with only a single dim sphere.

Gom is a character who brought a Shin vegetable to Ashir, which Ashir found fascinating as it caramelized. Ashir notes that Gom's contribution was significant enough that he is pleased to have received it.
A darkening, glassy expanse of water that is not as shallow as the Purelake, remains quite warm most of the time, and is dotted with tropical islands and occasional greatshell monsters.
Vevanev is a date marking the year 1173 and the time 107 seconds before a subject's death, recorded in the context of an out-of-work Veden sailor.
Leef is a character who was dozing and was addressed by a tall, red-haired man with incredulity.
Feverstone is the name of a red-rock fortification located within a natural rift, featuring blocky buildings and square raincatchers capable of withstanding a siege.
The Order of the Stonewards is a group of individuals who are currently on foot, accompanied by a large number of Windrunners.
Mashala is a character addressed by Adolin and Renarin, whom Adolin refers to as his favored aunt and who is formally addressed as such by Renarin.
Vevahach is a prostitute who died on the 11th of 1173, eight seconds before the date was recorded.
A creature that crawls around injured individuals, appearing alongside Kaladin and the members of Bridge Four as they look down on a wounded man from Eight.
Gibletish is a name suggested by a speaker for a Dysian Aimian that would be constructed by Dalinar from the emotional and physical fragments of a man.
Ialai is a curvaceous woman who reportedly dyed her hair, a trait indicating foreign blood in her family's past, as Alethi hair is said to always breed true.
Au-nak is a character who speaks with an airy accent and argues that money is the true driver behind every war, while religion serves only as an excuse or justification.
Nak-ali is a character addressed with the high form of Au-nak's name by Hatham, who distinguishes between an excuse and a justification in their conversation.

Sesemalex Dar is the capital city of the kingdom of Emul, described as an ancient and spectacular trade city featuring rifts cut into the stone, which is the subject of a war between the Emuli and the Tukari.

The Dawncities are a group of cities whose origins are traced back to the Dawnsingers, featuring unique stone compositions that allow water to drain. They are a subject of study for Hatham's wife and a topic of popular discussion among the lighteyes.
Tezim is the god-priest who leads the Tukari, a faction that believes the city's pattern is their ancestral homeland gifted by the Heralds.
The Order of Talenelat is a devotary for the lesser people, a religious group within the context of the provided text.

Brightlord Hatham is a highprince who instructs an ardent to insult Au-nak in order to make the foreigner believe he is shamed, thereby preventing suspicion over the quick agreement to Au-nak's demands. He is one of three highprinces, alongside Thanadal and Vamah, who are behind on payments, while only Highprince Sadeas has paid ahead.

A group of elite lighteyed officers who serve as personal bodyguards to Highprince Dalinar and Highprince Adolin, stepping up behind them during battle and maintaining a safe distance while their commanders engage the enemy.
A group or alliance mentioned by Highprince Sadeas, which Dalinar is considering abandoning.
Palahevan is a beggar of some renown, known for his elegant songs.
Brightking Alazansi is the ruler of the fleet consisting of a hundred ships, as referenced by the character Leyten.

Jezrien is the deity worshipped by the people of Sesemalex Dar, the capital of the kingdom of Emul, who regard him as their only god rather than a figure from the Vorin religion. The city and the kingdom are said to have been named by Jezrien himself.
Jezerezeh'elin is an alias for the Stormfather, who served as the king of the Heralds and is known for his mastery over storms, water, and life.
Nalma is a fellow slave who was lost by the protagonist during a previous escape attempt when the group was caught by their master's hunters.

Palahakev is the name of the date recorded on a document or inscription, as indicated by the phrase 'Dated Palahakev, 1173'. The provided text does not identify it as a person, faction, or object, but rather as a temporal marker for an event involving a Thaylen sailor.
Arik is a member of the bridge crew whose death, alongside Maps's, reduced the group's numbers to twenty-six, causing a noticeable heaviness and difficulty in keeping up with other crews.

Derethil was a powerful king during the shadowdays who commanded thousands of soldiers and fought the Voidbringers during the days of the Heralds and Radiants. After finding peace uncontenting, he commissioned a majestic ship intended to sail the seas during a highstorm.
The Wandersail is a ship that was nearly destroyed in a crash but survived with its crew, including Derethil and Nafti, who later escaped to a ring of islands surrounding an enormous whirlpool.
The Wander-sail is a vessel named by Derethil and crewed by the bravest sailors, which set out into the ocean to seek the origin of the Voidbringers on a day when a highstorm brewed.
The Great Abyss is a faction of people referred to as Uvara, who are characterized by their astonishing cruelty and a strict adherence to the will of their emperor, who will not suffer failure.

The Uvara are a group of people living on an island who, upon seeing a dead body, descend into chaos by burning homes and rioting. They possess shipyards where a vessel named the Wandersail is being repaired.
Nafti is a guide and caretaker who joins the crew of the Wandersail after begging to accompany Derethil and his men during their escape from the Uvara islands.
Shephir is the Midnight Mother, a terrifying figure who gives birth to abominations with an essence described as dark, terrible, and consuming. She is present and watching the narrator die.
Shashabev is a dark-skinned dock-worker in his forties and father of three who bears three brands on his forehead, including one labeled 'Shash' that marks him as dangerous.

The Outer Market is a disorganized collection of ramshackle stone buildings located near the king's palace and outside the warcamps, populated by an eclectic mix of people including many Thaylen merchants.
Jakamav is a sturdy lighteyes of the third dahn who serves as an accompaniment for Inkima and expresses a shared dislike for being wrong.
Eshava is a lighteye from Highprince Aladar's camp who accompanies Toral and is present in House Kholin's encampment, where she expresses dissatisfaction with Highprince Sadeas's restrictions.
Inkima is a character who laughs at Jakamav's comments and giggles when he whispers to her, while a serving maid brings her wine.
Brightlord Resi is a noble addressed by Danlan in a conversation regarding a potential response to vocal provocations from Thanadal.
Bajerden is an alias for the holy man Nohadon, whose real name is unknown and was likely lost in time. He is a historical figure who received a holy name from the Church before it was disbanded.
Natanatan, also known as the Granite Kingdom, is a legendary region located in the East of Roshar characterized by windswept plains, overgrown forests, and feral highstorms that rule supreme.

Resi is the only full Shardbearer in Highprince Thanadal's army and the holder of Resi's Shards, a title traditionally held by the Royal Defender in Thanadal's princedom. He is a combatant who faces Adolin in a duel where the objective is to shatter a section of the opponent's Shardplate.
Lady Istow serves as the highjudge who oversees a combat bout between Resi and Adolin, calling for the combatants to summon their Blades.
Stonestance is a specific fighting stance used by Resi that focuses on delivering maximum momentum and strength behind each strike, relying heavily on parrying postures.

The blade and plate is a set of ceremonial armor and a sword traditionally lent to highprinces or the king, representing a strong tradition within the kingdom that even former rulers like Gavilar respected despite private complaints.
Betabanan is the name of a specific date used in the calendar system of the lighteyed people, recorded as the date of a five-year-old child's death in 1173.
Chasmfiend is a specific term used by Kaladin to address a soldier, though the provided text does not define the nature of the entity or confirm if it is a person, object, or creature.
Betabanes is a date used to record events, specifically noted in a document dated 1173.
Longshadow is the creator of a specific type of early model device that Navani was refining. Navani suggests these creations have potential for use in battlefields to dull pain or assist surgeons.
Karm is a person addressed by a regal man who discusses unconventional thoughts regarding their abilities and divine election.
Surgebinders are a specific type of fabrial, a magical device, that are distinct from the bridge crews used by Highprince Dalinar's force.
Tarma is a location that has been devastated by the Desolations, with its people having fallen during the eleven years of war.
Eiliz is a kingdom that has been devastated by the Desolations, with its survival uncertain alongside Tarma due to the high number of fallen people.

Alakavish is a figure blamed by others for bringing the group to war before the Desolation, though he is described as merely a symptom of a greater disease rather than the sole cause of their suffering.
Corvana is the title of a volume written by an author named Corvana, which Navani retrieves from a shelf and examines on a writing desk.
The Silent Mount is a location in Jah Keved where the Vanrial, an order of artists, reside and sing ancient songs believed to be from the Dawnchant.

The Dawnchant is an ancient script and collection of songs written by the Heralds, containing words that have been preserved by the Vanrial order of artists on the Silent Mount in Jah Keved, though their original meanings have been lost to time.
Kakanev is the name of a city located in the Stormlight Archive universe, as indicated by the date 'Dated Kakanev, 1173' which marks a specific time and place before the death of a city guards-man.

The Desolations are a mysterious subject of inquiry for Dalinar and Renarin, regarding their connection to the Almighty and the Knights Radiant, though the specific nature of this group remains unproven in the provided text.

Parasaphi is a historical figure mentioned in a myth alongside Nadris, who is credited with searching for seed-stones. She is associated with a people whose existence was wiped out by the Desolations.

Nadris is a historical figure mentioned in the myth of Parasaphi and Nadris, who died on his deathbed after receiving stones touched by the Heralds. Navani harvested his seed to combine with these stones, resulting in the hatching of ten children used to found the new nation of Marnah.
Dara is a mythical mountain range that Navani climbed to find stones touched by the Heralds, which she used to hatch ten children and found the nation of Marnah.
Marnah is the name of a nation founded by Navani, who used ten children hatched from stones touched by the Heralds to repopulate her fallen people after harvesting the seed of Nadris.

A Shin Shardbearer is an individual who wields a Shard and wears Shard-plate armor, capable of using specialized weapons like grandbows that require such power to operate. This specific Shin Shardbearer is noted for assassinating the king of Jah Keved while wearing white clothing.
Avarak Matal is a man identified by Sadeas as looking familiar to Kaladin, though the provided text does not specify his role or relationship to the other characters.
Bridge Eighteen is one of the twenty bridge crews mentioned in the text, standing alongside Bridge Seven to observe Kaladin and his team after Matal checks on the other crews.
Tarah is a person whom the speaker considers a failure alongside others, specifically noting that she previously coaxed him out of his single-minded dedication.
Shshshsh is a character who is described as being loved by everyone, causing Navani to feel only mild jealousy rather than hatred.
The Black Fisher is a divine entity invoked by the speaker to consume their sorrow and end their weeping.
Bridge six is a specific crossing point located on a staging plateau where soldiers, including Kaladin's squad, are ordered to cross by Matal.
Dalinar's plate is a piece of armor that leaked Stormlight from dozens of small breaks, with light rising above it like steam from a cauldron and slowly diffusing.
A name shouted by groups of archers on a plateau, causing them to freeze and begin chanting a song in confusion.
Nelda is a person listed among the individuals Kaladin fears will die in the immediate future.
Tashlin is a Brightlord who commands a military company, and his reported death triggers a warning to Brightlord Amaram regarding the weakening forces on the east side.
Sheler is the name of a company of soldiers led by a Brightlord, who are holding their ground on the southeast side of the hill during the battle.
A banner displaying the glyphpair 'shesh lerel' that identifies Sheler's company, a military unit positioned on the southeast side of the hill.
Havar is a character who serves as the commander of the rear guard, a role confirmed by a man who calls out to him.
Nacomb Gaval is a character who appears to be younger than Kaladin.
Malan is the title held by a high-ranking military leader who commands soldiers and is addressed as 'Captainlord' by Adolin in a desperate plea to rescue his father.
Tanatanev is a character described as a dark-eyed mother of four in her sixty-second year who is facing death at 18 seconds before the event of 1173.
Navani is the widow of King Gavilar and the mother of Elhokar and Jasnah, who led research to develop grandbows using fabrial science.
Makal is an attendant who carries a satchel containing Navani's possessions, including her burn ink and brushpen.

Elthal is a distinguished, limber officer with a thin mustache who survived a disaster with his arm in a sling. He served as a high-ranking commander who held the gap alongside Dalinar during the final part of a fight and subsequently followed orders to tend to wounded horses.
A specialized discipline or training method that makes a character dangerous even without a Shardblade, allowing them to inflict severe injury on guards.
Vallano is the surname of Szeth, a man who killed Taravangian's friends and whom the king claims to share a heart with despite their violent differences.
The Lifebrother is a figure referenced by a speaker who claims to speak the listener's language and suggests the possibility that this entity sent the listener to them.
Szeth-son-son-Vallano is a man addressed by Taravangian as a fellow sinner who personally holds knives and releases blood, distinguishing him from those sent to do bloody work for others.
The True Desolation is a feared event or force that the figure believes will happen, which he states is what 'he' wants.
The Ghostbloods are a specific group searching for secrets, known to have assassinated the speaker's target and to have a member named Kabsal, who bore their symbol tattooed on his arm.
The Night of Sorrows is a cataclysmic event associated with the Everstorm and the True Desolation, described as a time when the sun approaches the horizon and the world faces a final threat.
Talenel'elin, also known as Stonesinew, is a Herald of the Almighty who believes he has failed as the Desolation has arrived.
Stonesinew is the name assumed by the man who identifies himself as Talenel'Elin, Herald of the Almighty, who believes he has failed to stop the Desolation.
